title: Adenocalymma pedunculatum
taxonomy: Adenocalymma pedunculatum is classified within the family Bignonaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further classified under the clade Asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Solanaceae and Lamiaceae. intro: Adenocalymma pedunculatum, also known as the "Brazilian cherry tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Adenocalymma pedunculatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
